english: sás-ka čòs* (sas ka chos) finish this work at once! - yul-k´ág (yul khag) province, district; rgyal-k´ág (rgyal khag) kingdom. - 3. W.: *k´ag* (khag) or *k´ág-gu tág-če* (khag gu btag ce) to hang (by the neck)

C. 1. difficult (W. *k´ág(s)-po* (khag(s) po)); hard (to bear), *k´ág-po jhuṅ* (khag po byung) it proved hard, *k´ág-po jhé-pa* (khag po byed pa) to suffer want. - 2. bad, spoiled, rotten, *mar k´ág-po soṅ* (mar khag po song) the butter has become rancid.
(?) Md.: *k´ág-la mar* (khag la mar) fresh butter, just made.
C.: vulg. f. *koṅ* (kong), sometimes also in books.

house, *k´aṅ-pa-la* (khang pa la) W. home, at home; in compounds also for a part of the house: room, story, floor etc., *steṅ-* (steng), *bár-* (bar), *óg-k´aṅ* (og khang) upper story, middle story, ground-floor Glr.; *bár-ma* (bar ma), *dkyil-ma* (dkyil ma) or *jzuṅ-k´aṅ* (gzhung khang) means also the usual dwelling-room, opp. to *p´ugs* (phugs) and *sgo* (sgo) (v. *sgo* (sgo)): *bzó-k´aṅ* (bzo khang) workshop; *báṅ-k´aṅ* (bang khang) store-house, store-room; *sgó-k´aṅ* (sgo khang) entrance, vestibule; *skór-k´aṅ* (skor khang) (Glr. 68, 9) seems to be a passage running round a building; *šóg-k´aṅ* (shog khang) W. the scooping-form or mould used in the manufacture of paper; *tsás-k´aṅ* (tsas khang) bed (garden). - *K´aṅ-glá* (khang gla) house-rent. - *k´aṅ-čuṅ* (khang chung) house or room reserved for decrepit parents; *k´aṅ-čuṅ-pa* (khang chung pa) inhabitant of such; *yaṅ-k´aṅ-čuṅ-pa* (yang khang chung pa) such a person of the second degree, (if, during his life, his son enters into the same right). - *k´aṅ-stóṅ* (khang stong) an empty house, which is thought a fit place for sorcery and necromancy. - *k´aṅ-bu* (khang bu) 1. little house, cottage. 2. room, *mya-ṅán-gyi k´.* (mya ngan gyi khang) room of mourning Dzl. - *k´aṅ-míg* (khang mig) room. - *k´aṅ-rtsá* (khang rtsa) foundation of a house Sch. - *k´aṅ-žábs* (khang zhabs) flooring of a room. - *k´aṅ-bzáṅs* (khang bzangs) residence, chiefly of gods. - *k´aṅ-rúl* (khang rul) Sch. a house in ruins.
1. litter, barrow. - 2. like, as, = *ltar* (ltar) Glr. - 3. = *k´od* (khod), *k´ad-snyóms* (khad snyoms) v. *k´od-snyóms* (khod snyoms).

1. the same as *k´ód-pa* (khod pa) to stick fast, to be seized, stopped, impeded, v. *k´ad-pa* (khad pa); hence also *ma-k´ád* (ma khad) = *ma-t´ág* (ma thag) as soon as: *dbugs čád ma-k´ád-du* (dbugs chad ma khad du) as soon as the breathing ceases Thgr.; *de ma-k´ád* (de ma khad) instantly, directly, *bu skyes-ma-k´ád čig* (bu skyes ma khad cig) Glr. a child born just now. - 2. to approach, draw near, with *la* (la), *nùb-la k´ád-pai tse* (nub la khad pai tshe) when the evening drew near Pth.; frq. with the perfect-root of a verb: *dbugs čád-la k´ád-pai dus* (dbugs chad la khad pai dus) when the ceasing of the breath approaches Thgr.; *zin-la k´ad yód-pa-la* (zin la khad yod pa la) as we were just about to seize him; *k´ád-du* (khad du) postpos. c.a.: *rtíṅ-pa k´ád-du* (rting pa khad du) as far as the heel Mil.; *k´ád-kyis* (khad kyis) adv. by degrees Mil.; *k´ád-la* (khad la), *k´ád-du* (khad du) id. Tar.
also *k´én-pa* (khen pa) 1. sbst. Schr.: wormwood, probably a mistake for *k´ám-pa* (kham pa). - 2. vb. to add (arithm.) Wdk.

more correctly *k´áṅ-da* (khang da), also spelled *k´á-dá* (kha da), Ssk, treacle or molasses partially dried, candy; *dé-la k´án-da bčos-pa* (de la khan da bcos pa) the candy made of it Med.; *skyér-k´an-da* (skyer khan da) candied *skyer-pa* (skyer pa).

1. court, residence of a prince, *rgyál-poi k´áb-kyi mi-rnams* (rgyal poi khab kyi mi rnams) courtiers. - 2. wife, spouse, *k´ab čén-ma* (khab chen ma) the first wife (in rank); *dé-la k´ab ós-pa ma rnyéd-nas* (de la khab os pa ma rnyed nas) as there was not found a wife worthy of him Glr.; *di gnyis ṅai k´áb-tu byuṅ-ba rmís-so* (di gnyis ngai khab tu byung ba rmis so) I dreamt that these two would become my wives Glr.; *k´áb-tu bžés-pa* (khab tu bzhes pa) to take for a wife, to marry. (Schr. has even a verb: *čúṅ-mar k´áb-pa* (chung mar khab pa).) - 3. needle, *k´ab-rtse* (khab rtse) point of a needle, *kab-rál*(?) (khab ral) Sch. needle-case, *k´ab-mig* (khab mig) eye of a needle, *k´áb-mig-tu skúd-pa jùg-pa* (khab mig tu skud pa 'jug pa) or *rgyúd-pa* (rgyud pa) to thread a needle; *p´ra-k´áb* (phra khab) a small needle, *sbom-k´áb* (sbom khab), *mo-k´áb* (mo khab) Dzl., *ta k´áb* (ta khab) W., *blo-k´áb* (blo khab) W., *k´ab-rúl* (khab rul) W. a large, thick needle, packing-needle; *k´ab-spú* (khab spu) bristle Sik.; *k´ab-lén (rdo)* (khab len (rdo)) loadstone, magnet.
